remember: Students will be able to recall and state the basic concepts of vectors, such as scalar and vector, magnitude and direction, types of vectors, and operations on vectors.
Students will be able to recall and apply the properties of vector addition, scalar multiplication, and dot product.
Students will be able to recall and solve problems involving vector equations of lines and planes.
understand: Students will be able to understand the geometric representation of vectors, including position vectors, and the concept of vector parallelogram.
Students will be able to understand the algebraic properties of vectors, including the commutative and distributive laws.
Students will be able to understand the concept of dot product and its applications.
apply: Students will be able to apply vector operations to solve problems involving vectors, including finding the resultant of two or more vectors.
Students will be able to apply the concept of vector equations of lines and planes to solve problems involving the intersection of lines and planes.
Students will be able to apply the properties of dot product to solve problems involving the angle between two vectors.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze and interpret the results of vector operations, including the direction and magnitude of resultant vectors.
Students will be able to analyze and interpret the results of vector equations of lines and planes, including the intersection of lines and planes.
Students will be able to analyze and interpret the results of dot product, including the angle between two vectors.

ncert_objectives: To understand the basic concepts of vectors, including scalar and vector, magnitude and direction, types of vectors, and operations on vectors.
To solve problems involving vector addition, scalar multiplication, and dot product.
To find the resultant of two or more vectors and the angle between two vectors.
board_exam_objectives: To solve problems involving vectors, including finding the resultant of two or more vectors.
To solve problems involving vector equations of lines and planes, including the intersection of lines and planes.
To solve problems involving the dot product, including the angle between two vectors.
